title: Detecting Non-Nil Tail in a Singly Linked List

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

hints.text: A linked list is nil if and only if it has no nodes.

editorial.title: Detecting Non-Nil Tail in a Singly Linked List
editorial.intuition: Check if the list has a tail element.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the list and check the last node.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a while loop to traverse the list.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a pointer to move to the tail of the list.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: If the list has a tail node, the function should return True.
editorial.dryRun: Consider examples of linked lists with and without tail nodes.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for the nil case
